verStmt.version: v1.1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution.
verStmt.notes:
Version 1:
Weights in General Household Survey 2014 v1 are based on figures provided by the 2013 mid-year population estimation model that incorporates the demographic findings of Census 2012. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
DataFirst added value labels to geotype variables in v1 person and house files. StatsSA released the data file without their value labels: 1 -“Urban formal” 2- “Urban informal” 4 “tribal areas” 5 “rural formal”
Version 1.1:
GHS 2014 version 1.1 includes revised weights. This version was released at the same time as GHS 2017 (21 June 2018). It was decided to replace the 2013 series mid-year population estimation in the previous version with a the more recent 2017 series mid-year population estimation as benchmarks for weighting the GHS data files. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
In the person file v1.1, the values for "personnr" 1-9 in v1 became 01-09 in v1.1.
In the house file v1.1, the value labels for q530cut was fixed.

stdyInfo.abstract: The GHS is an annual household survey specifically designed to measure the living circumstances of South African households. The GHS collects data on education, health and social development, housing, household access to services and facilities, food security, and agriculture.

sumDscr.geogCover: The General Household Survey 2014 had national coverage.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregations covered by the General Household Survey 2014 is Province.
sumDscr.universe: The survey covers all de jure household members (usual residents) of households in the nine provinces of South Africa and residents in workers' hostels. The survey does not cover collective living quarters such as student hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes:
The scope of the General Household Survey 2014 includes:
Household characteristics: Dwelling type, home ownership, access to water and sanitation, access to services, transport, household assets, land ownership, agricultural production Individuals' characteristics: demographic characteristics, relationship to household head, marital status, language, education, employment, income, health, fertility, disability, access to social services, mortality.

dataColl.sampProc:
The sample design for the GHS 2014 was based on a master sample (MS) that was originally designed for the Quarterly Labour Force Survey (QLFS) and was used for the first time for the GHS in 2008. This master sample is shared by the QLFS, GHS, Living Conditions Survey (LCS), Domestic Tourism Survey (DTS) and the Income and Expenditure Survey (IES).
The master sample used a two-stage, stratified design with probability-proportional-to-size (PPS) sampling of primary sampling units (PSUs) from within strata, and systematic sampling of dwelling units (DUs) from the sampled PSUs. A self-weighting design at provincial level was used and MS stratification was divided into two levels. Primary stratification was defined by metropolitan and non-metropolitan geographic area type. During secondary stratification, the Census 2001 data were summarised at PSU level. The following variables were used for secondary stratification: household size, education, occupancy status, gender, industry and income.
dataColl.weight:
The sampling weights for the data collected from the sampled households were constructed so that the responses could be properly expanded to represent the entire civilian population of South Africa. The design weights, which are the inverse sampling rate (ISR) for the province, are assigned to each of the households in a province. These were adjusted for four factors: Informal PSUs, Growth PSUs, Sample Stabilisation, and Non-responding Units.
Mid-year population estimates produced by the Demographic Analysis division were used for benchmarking. The final survey weights were constructed using regression estimation to calibrate to national level population estimates cross-classified by 5-year age groups, gender and race, and provincial population estimates by broad age groups. The 5-year age groups are: 0–4, 5–9, 10–14, 55–59, 60–64; and 65 and older. The provincial level age groups are 0–14, 15–34, 35–64; and 65 years and older. The calibrated weights were constructed in such away that all persons in a household would have the same final weight. These weights were later recalibrated.

anlyInfo.dataAppr:
GHS 2009-2015: The variable on land size in the General Household Survey questionnaire for 2009-2015 should be used with caution. The data comes from questions on the households' agricultural activities in Section 8 of the GHS questionnaire: Household Livelihoods: Agricultural Activities. Question 8.8b asks: “Approximately how big is the land that the household use for production? Estimate total area if more than one piece.” One of the response category is worded as: 1 = Less than 500m2 (approximately one soccer field) However, a soccer field is 5000 m2, not 500, therefore response category 1 is incorrect. The correct category option should be 5000 sqm. This response option is correct for GHS 2002-2008 and was flagged and corrected by Statistics SA in the GHS 2016.
GHS 2014-2018 and from GHS 2021 onwards: The person data file in has two Employment Status variables in the GHS. According to the Statistics SA documentation, both these variables are derived from the variables LAB1 and LAB12. But the value “Unspecified” in the variable employ_Status1 becomes “Not Economically Active” in the variable employ_Status2. There is no clear explanation for this change. Statistics SA has been contacted for further information on these variables.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2014 [dataset]. Version 1.1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2017. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2018. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/nkvx-r352
useStmt.conditions: Public use files, available to all

catgry.labl: Dwelling/house or brick/concrete block structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling servants' quarters/granny flat
Dwelling/house or brick/concrete block structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling servants' quarters/granny flat

var.name: Q53Floor
var.labl: Type of material used for the floor

var.name: Q55Open
var.labl: Open plan dining rooms/sitting rooms/TV rooms

catgry.labl: Owned, but not yet paid off to bank/financial institution
Rented from other (incl municipality and social housing institutions)
Owned, but not yet paid off to private lender
Piped (tap) water on site or in yard

var.name: Q513aDist
var.labl: Distance of water source from the dwelling

var.name: Q514Cle
var.labl: Clear - has no colour/ free of mud

var.name: Q518bREAS
var.labl: Reason household not paying for water

catgry.labl: Pay directly to landlord as part of rent
Permission from municipality not to pay
Water only delivered at fixed times
Flush toilet connected to a public sewerage system
Flush toilet connected to a septic tank

var.name: Q526WATER
var.labl: No water to flush the toilet

var.name: Q526MAINTANED
var.labl: Toilets not well maintained and broken

var.name: Q526ASSAULT
var.labl: Unsafe to use the toilet, due to risk of assault

var.name: Q526HEALTH
var.labl: Toilet unsafe to use, due to health risks

var.name: Q526STRUCTURE
var.labl: Toilet not enclosed well or structure damaged

var.name: Q526PIPES
var.labl: Broken pipes or blockages in the municipal system

var.name: Q526MANY
var.labl: Too many people, long waiting times

var.name: Q526TAP
var.labl: No tap or water point to wash hands after using the toilet

var.name: Q526REPAIRED
var.labl: Problem reported but not repaired within 5 working days

var.name: Q526Odours
var.labl: Toilet system not working properly causing odours and insects

catgry.labl: Connected to other source which household pays for
Connected to other source for which household is not paying for

var.name: Q529c1Avail
var.labl: Was the call centre available the first time

var.name: Q529c2Time
var.labl: Did you get a response within a reasonable time

var.name: Q529c3Solve
var.labl: Was the problem resolved in one call

catgry.labl: Removed by local authority/private company at least once a week
Removed by community members, contracted by the Municipality, less often than once a week
Removed by community members, contracted by the Municipality, at least once a week
Removed by community members, contracted by the Municipality, less often than once a week
Removed by community members at least once a week
Removed by community members less often than once a week

var.name: Q533bWill
var.labl: Payment for the removal of refuse or rubbish

var.name: Q535bDustbin
var.labl: Throw out into dustbin for refuse collection

var.name: Q535bNImportant
var.labl: Do not think it is important

var.name: Q535bTime
var.labl: Takes too much time to separate

var.name: Q535bDropoff
var.labl: Recycling dropoff points not conveniently located

catgry.labl: Fetched by companies contracted by municipality
Taken to drop off point by household

var.name: Q535gEasy
var.labl: Because a recycling service is easily accessible

var.name: Q535gSupport
var.labl: To support a community/school recycling programme

var.name: Q62bNrCell
var.labl: Total number of cellular telephone in the household

var.name: Q64Int2
var.labl: Internet in a library or community hall/Thusong centre

var.name: Q64Int3
var.labl: Internet for students at a school/university/college

var.name: Q64Int5
var.labl: Internet Café 2 km or less from the household

var.name: Q64Int6
var.labl: Internet Café more than 2 km from the household

var.name: Q64Int7
var.labl: Any place via a mobile cellular phone

var.name: Q64Int8
var.labl: Any place via any other mobile access services

var.name: Q66RSNINT
var.labl: Reasons for not having internet access at home

catgry.labl: Concern about exposure to inappropriate or harmful contents
Delivered to a post box/private bag
Through friend or neighbour or relative

var.name: Q610Dttr
var.labl: Dist (travel to get to the nearest bus stop)

catgry.labl: Drugs that were needed, not available
Staff rude or uncaring or turned patient away
Not on medical aid scheme list of facilities
Prefer to use a state/provincial health institution
Prefer to use a private health institution

var.name: Q78STRK
var.labl: Young person living on the streets

var.name: Q79MF
var.labl: Run out of money to buy food

var.name: Q79MF5
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: Q710Meal
var.labl: Cut the size of meal or skip any meals

var.name: Q710Meal5
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: Q711Less5
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: Q712Lfd5
var.labl: Has it happened 5 or more days in the past 30 days

var.name: Q713MAIZE
var.labl: Maize, rice, sorghum, millet, bread and other

var.name: Q713BEAN
var.labl: Beans, peas, groundnuts, cashew nuts or other types of nuts

var.name: Q713OTHVEG
var.labl: Other vegetables, carrots, relish, tomatoes, cabbage, beetroot etc

var.name: Q713MEAT
var.labl: Beef, goat, poultry (chicken), pork, fish, eggs, lamb

var.name: Q713dairy
var.labl: Milk, yoghurt and other dairy products

var.name: Q83Fod
var.labl: Fodder grazing/pasture or grass for animals

var.name: Q84Use
var.labl: Use of agricultural products and stock keeping

catgry.labl: As a main source of food for the household
As the main source of income/earning a living
As an extra source of income
As an extra source of food for the household
As a leisure activity or hobby e.g. gardening

var.name: Q85aSell
var.labl: Agricultural products sold by the households

var.name: Q85bSProd
var.labl: Buyers of the agricultural products sold

catgry.labl: Buyers from neighbouring cities and towns

var.name: Q86aGrnt
var.labl: Grants (money that does not have to be paid back)

var.name: Q86aLoan
var.labl: Loans (money that has to be paid back)

var.name: Q86aInpL
var.labl: Inputs (seed, fertilizer, etc.) as part of a loan

var.name: Q86aDip
var.labl: Dipping and vaccination services for livestock

var.name: Q86CAnyASSIST
var.labl: Agriculture-related assistance from any other entity than government

var.name: Q88aVRd
var.labl: On verges of roads and unused public/municipal land

var.name: Q88bHect
var.labl: Size of the land that the household use

catgry.labl: Less than 500 m2 (approximately one soccer field)
500 m2 to 9 999 m2 (between one soccer field and one hectare)

var.name: Q89aAgric
var.labl: Sales of farm products and services

var.name: Q89aOth
var.labl: Other income sources, e.g. rental income, interest

catgry.labl: Sales of farm products and services
Other income sources e.g. rental income, interest

var.name: Q812Netincome
var.labl: Net household income per month in Rand

var.name: Q813Mincome
var.labl: Monthly income of your household higher

var.name: Q817DomW
var.labl: Domestic or household worker's services used by the household

var.name: Q819Status
var.labl: you and your household are at present

var.name: Q820Happy
var.labl: happier, the same or less happy with life

var.name: Q822Per
var.labl: person who answered most of the questions in section 5-8

var.name: Q92Membersdied
var.labl: Members of the household who passed away

catgry.labl: Other relative (e.g. in-laws or aunt/uncle) of person 01
Living together like husband and wife
Single, but have been living together with someone as husband and/wife before
Single and have never been married/never lived together as husband/wife before
Post Higher Diploma (Technikon/University of Technology Masters, Doctoral)

var.name: Q16ATECHILD
var.labl: Type of ECD facility children attend

var.name: Q17DA
var.labl: Place where children spent most of their time during the day

catgry.labl: At home with parent, foster parent or guardian
At home with someone younger than 18 years
Has completed education/satisfied with my level of education/do not want to study
He or she is working at home or business/job
Education is useless or not interesting
Pre-school (including day care, crèche, pre-primary, ECD centre,
School (including Grade R/Grade 0 learners who attended a formal
Adult Education and Training Learning Centre (AET Centre)
Other than any of the above
Minibus/bus provided by institution/government and not paid for
Vehicle hired by a group of parents
Own car or other private vehicle

var.name: Q115dNNI
var.labl: Reason not attending the nearest institution

var.name: Q116FEET
var.labl: Total amount of tuition fees paid

var.name: Q119cTABS
var.labl: Teachers are often absent from school

var.name: Q119cPMAN
var.labl: Teachers were involved in a strike

catgry.labl: Yes, a few times a week

var.name: Q123aVIOL
var.labl: Violence corporal punishment or verbal abuse

catgry.labl: Not applicable - school closed e.g. school holiday
Did not want to go to school
Need to take care of someone else at home
Does not feel safe at school

var.name: Q23FLU
var.labl: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection

var.name: Q23SCblood
var.labl: TB or severe cough with blood

catgry.labl: Not necessary/the problem was not serious enough
Experienced difficulty getting a diagnosis before

var.name: Q25aSTR
var.labl: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating

catgry.labl: Does not go to school or work
The child has been born alive
The child died in the womb or during childbirth after the 7th month of pregnancy (stillbirth)
The child died in the womb or the pregnancy ended before the 7th month of pregnancy (spontaneous abortion/miscarriage)
The pregnancy was ended by choice before the child was born (termination of pregnancy/abortion by choice)

var.name: Q41aWGE
var.labl: Working for a wage, commission or salary

catgry.labl: Lift club by a group of people sharing a private vehicle

var.name: Q44bMIN
var.labl: Time taken to place of employment

catgry.labl: In the formal sector (registered to perform activity)
In the informal sector (not registered to perform activity)

var.name: Q46aLOOK
var.labl: Looking for any kind of job or trying business

var.name: Q46cRNSW
var.labl: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business

var.name: Q47JCR
var.labl: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme

catgry.labl: Acentral, tribal, animist, or other traditional African religions
Usually at least once a week
Usually once or twice a month
Usually a few times a year
